summary: Ensures all CI‑OS activities comply with safety, regulatory, and organizational standards while proactively identifying and mitigating operational risks.
responsibilities: Develop, maintain, and enforce safety and compliance policies across all CI‑OS processes
Conduct safety audits, compliance inspections, and risk assessments
Investigate safety incidents, near misses, and nonconformities
Collaborate with Process Engineers to ensure process changes meet safety and compliance requirements
Monitor regulatory updates and ensure organizational alignment with new standards
Maintain safety training materials in partnership with the Training & Knowledge Manager
Track and analyze safety metrics, incident trends, and compliance performance
Approve safety-critical changes and ensure proper documentation
Lead emergency response planning and drills
Ensure safe integration of automation, digital systems, and digital-twin simulations
authorities: Suspend operations in case of safety-critical risks
Approve or reject process changes based on safety impact
Define safety standards and compliance thresholds
Initiate formal investigations into safety incidents
kaizenCapabilities: Identification of safety-related waste and hazards
Coaching teams on safe improvement practices
Participation in gemba walks with a safety lens
cqiCapabilities: Integration of safety metrics into CQI cycles

constraints: All safety decisions must comply with regulatory standards
Incident investigations must be documented and traceable
Safety overrides take precedence over operational efficiency
